student_message: Wait, before we do the V, can you tell me what the capital of France is?
final_message: The capital of France is Paris! But let's take a quick trip back to our math magic and the letter V. I have a fun riddle for you to get us right back on track. 🦋 I am an invisible line that makes a shape twin. If you fold a paper on me, both sides perfectly match to win! A butterfly wears me right down its back. Can you guess what I am? Once you guess my name, we can see if the letter V has one of these magical lines too! 🤔

orchestrator_intent.goal: Redirect the student back to the topic of symmetry and the letter V.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student is asking an off-topic question about geography. The engagement agent is needed to gently redirect focus back to the math lesson.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
